epic.title: ADO Command Center
epic.description: Control plane for scrum execution across the EVA Platform. Reads ADO board, identifies active work items, dispatches project-specific copilot-agent runners. ADO is the system of record for work. GitHub is the system of record for code. Azure is the runtime. Copilot Cloud Agent is the developer acceleration layer.

features.title: ADO Infrastructure & Scripts
features.description: ADO project setup, iteration management, work item hierarchy creation and closing scripts. ado-setup.ps1, ado-bootstrap-pull.ps1, ado-close-wi.ps1, ado-create-bug.ps1.

features.title: GitHub-ADO Async Bridge
features.description: ADO Pipeline fires workflow_dispatch to GitHub. GitHub Actions runs DPDCA. Three-event model: sprint start, WI heartbeats, sprint complete. Observability: 6-layer monitoring stack.

features.title: Project Onboarding Workflow
features.description: cross-06-ado-project-onboard skill. Scans eva-foundation numbered project folders, classifies maturity, produces ado-artifacts.json + ado-import.ps1 for each project.

features.title: ADO Idea Intake Pipeline
features.description: Self-service work item creation from repo docs. Developer drops README/PLAN/ACCEPTANCE into docs/ADO/idea/. Skill 07 reads the files, generates ado-artifacts.json, GitHub Action imports to ADO. PR bridge (ado-pr-bridge.yml) keeps WI state in sync with PR lifecycle. ADO webhook bridge Azure Function propagates ADO events back to GitHub. Three-system model fully wired.

features.title: Dependency-Aware Thread Dispatch
features.description: Elevate the command center from work-item dispatch to dependency-aware thread orchestration. Builds dispatch manifests, resolves ready queues, applies concurrency groups, and opens governed activation gates for cloud agents.

user_stories.title: [ADO-WI-0] ADO project eva-poc scaffolded — Epic, Features, Sprints, Scripts
user_stories.acceptance_criteria: Org eva-poc created with Scrum process. Epic id=4, Feature id=5 (Brain), id=6 (Faces). Sprint-0 through Sprint-6 created and team-assigned. All 4 PowerShell scripts operational. PAT never stored.

evidence.notes: Completed 2026-02-20. Verified via PowerShell REST query.

user_stories.title: [ADO-WI-1] GitHub org eva-foundry connected to ADO eva-poc via GitHub App
user_stories.acceptance_criteria: GitHub App connection exists in ADO Project Settings > GitHub connections. eva-foundry org linked. At minimum 14-az-finops + 1 repo verified as linked.

user_stories.title: [ADO-WI-2] All 18 eva-foundation projects onboarded to ADO — ado-artifacts.json + import complete
user_stories.acceptance_criteria: ado-artifacts.json exists in all 18 numbered project folders. ado-onboard-all.ps1 shows all 18 as imported. Board reflects correct maturity, sprints, and WI hierarchy per project.

evidence.notes: ACTIVE. Artifacts generated 2026-02-21. Pending review + import execution.

user_stories.title: [ADO-WI-3] GitHub-ADO async bridge — sprint-execute.yml, watchdog-poll.yml, morning-summary.yml
user_stories.acceptance_criteria: sprint-execute.yml fires on workflow_dispatch from ADO Pipeline. WI heartbeat variable SPRINT_HEARTBEAT updated every 10 min. Watchdog fires ADO WARNING at 25 min stall. Morning summary posts at 07:00 ET.

evidence.notes: Templates created in .github/workflows/. Ready to copy to project repos.

user_stories.title: [ADO-WI-4] Idea intake pipeline — skill 07, templates, ado-generate-artifacts.ps1, ado-idea-intake.yml
user_stories.acceptance_criteria: Skill 07-ado-idea-intake.skill.md exists in 29-foundry. README/PLAN/ACCEPTANCE.md.template created. ado-generate-artifacts.ps1 parses three files and writes valid ado-artifacts.json. ado-idea-intake.yml triggers on docs/ADO/idea/ push, commits artifact, posts PR comment.

evidence.notes: Completed 2026-02-21. All files created this session.

user_stories.title: [ADO-WI-5] Three-system wiring — ado-pr-bridge.yml + ado-webhook-bridge Azure Function
user_stories.acceptance_criteria: ado-pr-bridge.yml: PR open→Approved, review→Committed, merge→Done with evidence comment. ado-webhook-bridge Function deployed. ADO webhook subscription created for workitem.updated events. CI failure comment posted to ADO WI. Integration smoke test: open a PR with [WI-ID:N], verify state transitions in ADO.

evidence.notes: Files created 2026-02-21. Needs: GITHUB_TOKEN secret, ADO_PAT secret, Azure Function deployment, ADO webhook registration.

user_stories.title: [ADO-WI-6] Dispatch manifest synthesis — convert backlog and dependencies into executable thread packets
user_stories.acceptance_criteria: 38-ado-poc produces a dependency-aware dispatch manifest per sprint packet. Manifest identifies thread ids, parent-child relationships, blockers, lane assignments, and activation prerequisites for each executable thread.

evidence.notes: Moves 38 from work-item dispatch to thread-graph dispatch.

user_stories.title: [ADO-WI-7] Ready queue and concurrency groups — run dependent and independent lanes systematically
user_stories.acceptance_criteria: Command Center computes ready queue, blocked queue, and concurrency groups from the thread graph. Independent lanes can run in parallel while dependent lanes remain gated until prerequisites pass.

evidence.notes: Core scheduling behavior for the control plane.

user_stories.title: [ADO-WI-8] Activation gates and handoff orchestration — open, pause, retry, and hand off thread execution
user_stories.acceptance_criteria: 38-ado-poc supports governed activation gates, human approval holds, retry routing, and handoff targets for each thread packet. Gate decisions are exported to 40 and 19 surfaces.

evidence.notes: Required for multi-thread execution under governance.
